content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All three amendments are verified against the source code. Amendments fr-5-66317fda and fr-5-293b5fe0 identify the same real issue: run.json lines 33-36 contain a `final_review_planner` field that no longer exists in the StageResolutionSnapshot struct (model.rs:186-207) and is never emitted by build_final_review_snapshot (engine.rs:8206-8223). This is stale data that violates the current schema contract. Amendment fr-5-aa018b94 identifies a genuine test correctness bug: all four voter sequences in scenarios.rs still include 'Planner position.' entries (confirmed at lines 12795, 12938, 13052, 13216, 13232), which cause an off-by-one payload misalignment when the planner is no longer consuming entries from the sequence. The multi-round test is particularly affected — reviewer 0 in round 2 receives 'Reviewer 3 vote' targeting round 1's amendment_id, silently reducing effective accept count. All three amendments are accepted.

## Votes

- `fr-5-66317fda`: Accept
  Verified: run.json lines 33-36 contain `final_review_planner` but the StageResolutionSnapshot struct (model.rs:186-207) has no such field — grep confirms zero matches for `final_review_planner` in model.rs. The builder function build_final_review_snapshot (engine.rs:8206-8223) only populates final_review_reviewers and final_review_arbiter. The persisted data is schema-non-conformant and should be cleaned up to match the current struct definition.
- `fr-5-aa018b94`: Accept
  Verified: all four voter payload sequences contain 'Planner position.' entries (lines 12795, 12938, 13052, 13216, 13232). With the planner removed from the voting flow, reviewers consume these payloads sequentially, causing a systematic off-by-one. The multi-round restart_cap_force_complete test is most impacted: it has 8 entries (planner+3 per round) but only 3 reviewers per round consume entries, so reviewer 0 in round 2 gets 'Reviewer 3 vote' which references round 1's amendment_id instead of second_round_amendment_id. The vote aggregation silently drops this mismatched vote, reducing effective accept count from 3 to 2. Tests pass only because 2/3 still meets the acceptance threshold. This is a real correctness bug that could mask vote aggregation regressions.
- `fr-5-293b5fe0`: Accept
  This is the same substantive finding as fr-5-66317fda, independently discovered and verified. run.json active_run.stage_resolution_snapshot (lines 33-36) serializes `final_review_planner`, but the authoritative schema in StageResolutionSnapshot (model.rs:186-207) contains only `final_review_reviewers` and `final_review_arbiter` for final-review stages. The stale field in the data artifact violates the no-planner snapshot contract established by the code changes.
